title: Sarga 75
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Then, having heard those nectar-like words of Narada, Rama obtained immeasurable joy and spoke this to Lakshmana.” through “Having bowed, the Pushpaka spoke, "O king of men, O mighty-armed one, I am your obedient servant, present here."” through “Seated in Pushpaka, the mighty-armed king then saw (the entire land) whose conduct was perfectly pure and as clear as the surface of a mirror.”

Themes: self-knowledge, freedom from attachment, virtue and right conduct. The passage moves from “Then, Rama, the son of the royal sage, proceeded towards the southern direction and on the northern side of Shaivala, he saw a very large lake.” through “"O venerable ascetic, in which birth or species do you exist, O one of firm resolve? I ask you out of curiosity, for I am Rama, the son of…” through “Thus addressed by the king, the ascetic, with his head bowed, revealed his caste and the reason for his severe penance to Rama, the best of…”

verses.sanskrit: नारदस्य तु तद्वाक्यं श्रुत्वा ऽमृतमयं तदा । प्रहर्षमतुलं लेभे लक्ष्मणं चेदमब्रवीत् ।। 7.75.1 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having heard those nectar-like words of Narada, Rama obtained immeasurable joy and spoke this to Lakshmana.

verses.sanskrit: गच्छ सौम्य द्विजश्रेष्ठं समाश्वासय सुव्रतम् । बालस्य तु शरीरं तत्तैलद्रोण्यां निधापय ।। 7.75.2 ।।
verses.translation: O gentle one, go and console that best of Brahmins, the one of good vows, and have the child's body preserved in a trough of oil.

verses.sanskrit: गन्धैश्च परमोदारैस्तैलैश्चापि सुगन्धिभिः । यथा न क्षीयते बालस्तथा सौम्य विधीयताम् ।। 7.75.3 ।।
verses.translation: O gentle one, let it be done with very excellent fragrances and fragrant oils so that the child does not waste away.

verses.sanskrit: यथा शरीरो बालस्य गुप्तः सन् शिष्टकर्मणः । विपत्तिः परिभेदो वा न भवेच्च तथा कुरु ।। 7.75.4 ।।
verses.translation: Ensure that the body of the well-behaved child is protected so that no misfortune or injury may occur.

verses.sanskrit: एवमादिश्य काकुत्स्थो लक्ष्मणं शुभलक्षणम् । मनसा पुष्पकं दध्यावागच्छेति महायशाः ।। 7.75.5 ।।
verses.translation: Having thus instructed the auspicious-featured Lakshmana, the highly renowned Rama thought of the Pushpaka chariot in his mind, commanding it to come.

verses.sanskrit: इङ्गितं स तु विज्ञाय पुष्पको हेमभूषितः । आजगाम मुहूर्तेन समीपे राघवस्य वै ।। 7.75.6 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, having understood Rama's mental signal, the gold-adorned Pushpaka chariot arrived near Rama in a moment.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽब्रवीत्प्रणतो भूत्वा अयमस्मि नराधिप । वश्यस्तव महाबाहो किङ्करः समुपस्थितः ।। 7.75.7 ।।
verses.translation: Having bowed, the Pushpaka spoke, "O king of men, O mighty-armed one, I am your obedient servant, present here."

verses.sanskrit: भाषितं रुचिरं श्रुत्वा पुष्पकस्य नराधिपः । अभिवाद्य महर्षींस्तान् विमानं सो ऽध्यरोहत ।। 7.75.8 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the pleasing words of Pushpaka, the king (Rama) saluted those great sages and then ascended the aerial chariot.

verses.sanskrit: धनुर्गृहीत्वा तूणी च खड्गं च रुचिरप्रभम् । निक्षिप्य नगरे वीरौ सौमित्रिभरतावुभौ ।। 7.75.9 ।।
verses.translation: Having taken his bow, quiver, and a beautifully shining sword, and having left both heroes Lakshmana and Bharata in the city, (Rama proceeded).

verses.sanskrit: प्रायात्प्रतीचीं हरितं विचिन्वंश्च ततस्ततः । उत्तरामगमच्छ्रीमान्दिशं हिमवता वृताम् ।। 7.75.10 ।।
verses.translation: The glorious Rama proceeded to the western direction, searching everywhere, and then went to the northern direction, which is covered by the Himalayas.

verses.sanskrit: अपश्यमानस्तत्रापि स्वल्पमप्यथ दुष्कृतम् । पूर्वामपि दिशं सर्वामथो ऽपश्यन्नराधिपः ।। 7.75.11 ।।
verses.translation: Not finding even a slight evil deed there (in the north), the king then surveyed the entire eastern direction as well.
Seated in Pushpaka, the mighty-armed king then saw (the entire land) whose conduct was perfectly pure and as clear as the surface of a mirror.

verses.sanskrit: दक्षिणां दिशमाक्रामत्ततो राजर्षिनन्दनः । शैवलस्योत्तरे पार्श्वे ददर्श सुमहत्सरः ।। 7.75.13 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Rama, the son of the royal sage, proceeded towards the southern direction and on the northern side of Shaivala, he saw a very large lake.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्सरसि तप्यन्तं तापसं सुमहत्तपः । ददर्श राघवः श्रीमाँल्लम्बमानमधोमुखम् ।। 7.75.14 ।।
verses.translation: In that lake, the glorious Rama saw an ascetic performing very great penance, hanging head downwards.

verses.sanskrit: राघवस्तमुपागम्य तप्यन्तं तप उत्तमम् । उवाच स तदा वाक्यं धन्यस्त्वमसि सुव्रत ।। 7.75.15 ।।
verses.translation: Rama, having approached him who was performing excellent penance, then spoke these words: "O ascetic of good vows, you are blessed."

verses.sanskrit: कस्यां योन्यां तपोवृद्ध वर्तसे दृढविक्रम । कौतूहलात्त्वां पृच्छामि रामो दाशरथिर्ह्यहम् ।। 7.75.16 ।।
verses.translation: "O venerable ascetic, in which birth or species do you exist, O one of firm resolve? I ask you out of curiosity, for I am Rama, the son of Dasharatha."

verses.sanskrit: को ऽर्थो मनीषितस्तुभ्यं स्वर्गलाभः परो ऽथवा । वराश्रयो यदर्थं त्वं तपस्यसि सुदुष्करम् । यमाश्रित्य तपस्तप्तं श्रोतुमिच्छामि तापस ।। 7.75.17 ।।
verses.translation: "What is the purpose desired by you, is it the attainment of heaven or some other supreme boon, for which you are performing this very difficult penance, O ascetic? I wish to hear what you have resorted to for performing this penance."

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणो वा ऽसि भद्रं ते क्षत्रियो वा ऽसि दुर्जयः । वैश्यस्तृतीयवर्णो वा शूद्रो वा सत्यवाग्भव ।। 7.75.18 ।।
verses.translation: Rama asks the ascetic to truthfully state his varna, whether he is a Brahmana, a Kshatriya, a Vaishya, or a Shudra, wishing him well regardless.

verses.sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्तः स नराधिपेन ह्यवाक्छिरा दाशरथाय तस्मै । उवाच जातिं नृपपुङ्गवाय यत्कारणे चैव तपःप्रयत्नः ।। 7.75.19 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed by the king, the ascetic, with his head bowed, revealed his caste and the reason for his severe penance to Rama, the best of kings. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे पञ्चसप्ततितमः सर्गः ।। 75 ।। Thus ends the seventy-fifth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
